# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript, serving as the user interface for a comprehensive HR management system. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Application Structure
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Routing**: React Router for client-side navigation
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, custom hooks for local state
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and Shadcn/ui components
- **Backend Integration**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions)

### Key Architectural Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Shared logic extracted into reusable hooks
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for global state (auth, notifications)
- **Compound Components**: Complex UI patterns like forms and modals
- **Higher-Order Components**: For cross-cutting concerns like permissions

## Core Business Logic

### Employee Management
- Employee profiles with comprehensive data tracking
- Role-based access control with granular permissions
- Department and position management
- Employee lifecycle management (onboarding, transfers, termination)

### Attendance System
- Automated attendance tracking with Excel import
- AI-powered data parsing for various attendance formats
- Overtime calculation based on shift configurations
- Late arrival detection and reporting
- Weekend and holiday handling

### Leave Management
- Multiple leave types with balance tracking
- Approval workflows with hierarchical permissions
- Leave balance calculations and carry-forward rules
- Integration with attendance for absence tracking

### Payroll Processing
- Automated payroll generation
- Overtime and allowance calculations
- Salary history and increment tracking
- Tax and deduction handling

### Performance Management
- Structured evaluation processes
- Custom evaluation parameters
- Review scheduling and reminders
- Performance history tracking

## Key Files and Components

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Dashboard/home page

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout with sidebar navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Brand logo with variant support
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced navigation with active state styling

### Feature Components
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceUploadFlow.tsx`: Complex attendance import workflow
- `src/components/employee-profile/AttendanceTab.tsx`: Attendance records display
- `src/components/leave/ApplyLeaveModal.tsx`: Leave request form
- `src/components/payroll/PayrollSummary.tsx`: Payroll data visualization
- `src/components/settings/CompanyTab.tsx`: Company configuration

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Time and duration formatting
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending logic

### Hooks
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/hooks/useToast.ts`: Toast notification system
- `src/hooks/useSort.ts`: Table sorting functionality

### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions

## Data Flow and State Management

### Server State
- React Query manages all API interactions
- Automatic caching, background refetching, and optimistic updates
- Query keys follow a hierarchical structure for invalidation

### Local State
- Component-level state for UI interactions
- Form state managed by React Hook Form
- Modal and dialog state

### Global State
- Authentication state via context
- User preferences and settings
- Notification state

## Database Schema (Key Tables)

### Core Entities
- `employees`: Employee master data
- `companies`: Multi-tenant company information
- `departments`: Organizational structure
- `roles`: Permission roles

### HR Operations
- `attendance_records`: Daily attendance data
- `leave_requests`: Leave applications and approvals
- `payroll_records`: Payroll processing data
- `evaluations`: Performance reviews

### Configuration
- `company_settings`: Company-wide configurations
- `leave_types`: Configurable leave categories
- `evaluation_parameters`: Custom evaluation criteria

## Authentication and Authorization

### Authentication
- Supabase Auth for user management
- Email/password authentication
- Session persistence and refresh

### Authorization
- Role-based access control
- Permission checking at component and route levels
- Hierarchical permissions (admin > manager > employee)

### Security Features
- Row-level security in Supabase
- API key protection for sensitive operations
- Audit logging for user actions

## API Patterns

### Supabase Integration
- Direct table queries with RLS
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Storage API for file uploads
- Edge functions for complex processing

### Data Fetching
- React Query for declarative data fetching
- Error handling and loading states
- Optimistic updates for better UX

### File Processing
- Client-side Excel parsing with SheetJS
- AI-powered data normalization
- Batch processing for large datasets

## UI/UX Patterns

### Design System
- Tailwind CSS with custom color palette
- Consistent spacing and typography
- Component variants for different use cases
- Dark/light theme support

### Component Patterns
- Compound components for complex UIs
- Render props for customization
- Controlled components with proper state management
- Accessibility-first approach

### User Experience
- Progressive loading and skeleton states
- Toast notifications for feedback
- Modal dialogs for complex interactions
- Responsive design for all screen sizes

## Development Workflow

### Build and Deployment
- Vite for fast development builds
- TypeScript compilation with strict checks
- ESLint for code quality
- Automated testing with Vitest

### Code Organization
- Feature-based folder structure
- Shared components in ui/ directory
- Utility functions in lib/ directory
- Type definitions with Supabase generated types

### Performance Considerations
- Code splitting by routes
- Lazy loading of heavy components
- Image optimization and CDN usage
- Database query optimization

## Common Patterns and Conventions

### Naming Conventions
- PascalCase for components
- camelCase for functions and variables
- kebab-case for CSS classes
- UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants

### Error Handling
- Try/catch blocks for async operations
- User-friendly error messages
- Fallback UI states
- Logging for debugging

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for components
- E2E tests for critical user flows
- Mock data for isolated testing

This technical context provides a comprehensive understanding of the Beudox HR application's architecture, patterns, and implementation details to assist with development, debugging, and maintenance tasks.
